sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Routine follow-up with chronic medical conditions and fatigue.
History of Present Illness
The patient presents today for a routine follow-up with chronic medical conditions. He reports increased fatigue, particularly at the end of the day, but continues to ride his bike, though at a slower pace. He notes that he can only ride approximately 20-30 miles, whereas he used to be able to ride 40 miles. He mentions a palpable nodule in his right testicle, which he has been monitoring. He states that his wife has observed pursed lip breathing during exhalation. He is currently taking vitamin D3 and clindamycin prior to dental procedures. He has received both doses of the COVID-19 vaccine. He underwent an aortic valve replacement and ascending aortic aneurysm repair on 1/22/2013.
Findings
Examination reveals pursed lip breathing and a low exhalation phase. The right testicle is palpable with a two to three millimeter nodule. The remainder of the physical exam is unremarkable. Lab results show an elevated alkaline phosphatase level of 156.
Assessment
The patient's fatigue and elevated alkaline phosphatase level require further investigation. The nodule in the right testicle warrants referral to urology.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Order an echocardiogram and stress test. Order pulmonary function testing. Refer the patient to urology for evaluation of the testicular nodule. Order lab work to evaluate the alkaline phosphatase level. Schedule a follow-up in one year for a wellness visit, sooner if needed. Recommend the shingles vaccine.
